finding.summary: Stereo pad plays back with a -4.1 dB L/R imbalance despite a centered pan control; this is content-level asymmetry, not a routing problem. The three reported 1/3-octave bands show a steady ~6 dB/oct rolloff from 100 Hz to 4 kHz (dark tilt), but the spectrum snapshot is too sparse to act on. Loudness (-20 LUFS), true peak (-5.5 dBTP), crest factor (12 dB) and LRA (8 LU) are all healthy for a pad; no FX chain is loaded.
finding.probable_cause: The pad's underlying audio has built-in L/R asymmetry (typical of layered or stereo-widened pad patches where two sources are not perfectly level-matched). The track pan is at 0.0 so REAPER reproduces the imbalance faithfully. The dark tilt in the three measured bands could be source-patch voicing, but with bands below 100 Hz and above 4 kHz unreported, it is not safe to call the overall tonal balance dark.
finding.confidence_reason: Stereo balance is a direct, unambiguous measurement. Correlation 0.74 with side RMS 6 dB below mid is consistent with a moderately wide but still center-dominant pad, supporting the 'imbalance is baked into the content' reading. Tonal diagnosis is low confidence because only three 1/3-octave points are reported; everything outside 100 Hz–4 kHz is unknown.

evidence_refs.description: -4.1 dB — L is louder than R
0.0 — pan control is centered, so the bias is in the source, not the routing
0.74 — moderately correlated, pad is still center-dominant
Only 100 Hz / 1 kHz / 4 kHz reported at -18 / -24 / -30 dB; insufficient for an EQ recommendation
0.05 — measurements are not diluted by silence
Empty — no corrective M/S or EQ processor available

proposal.reason: Pan slightly right to compensate for the -4.1 dB L-bias in the source. Exact amount depends on the project's stereo pan law (not reported in the payload): with REAPER's default -3 dB-center equal-power law a small right pan yields ~1–2 dB of recentering, while a 0 dB-center linear law moves more for the same value. +0.20 is a conservative starting point — verify by re-measuring audio.stereo.balance_db and nudge further toward +0.25…+0.35 if needed. A pan move cannot perfectly reconstruct center if the source asymmetry is non-gain-only (e.g. different material L vs R), but for a steady pad it will perceptually recenter the image. Cannot verify improvement without a before/after re-measurement.
